# SpaceCommsKit — llms.txt
# https://llmstxt.org

# SpaceCommsKit produces the SpaceCommsKit Explorer SCK-915 — an RF communications
# development kit inspired by the open-source OpenLST design released by Planet Labs.
# OpenLST inspired. SCK-915 evolved. Open-source software, proprietary hardware.
# Hand-assembled in Tennessee, US only.

## About SpaceCommsKit

SpaceCommsKit produces the SpaceCommsKit Explorer SCK-915, a complete RF communications
development kit inspired by and building upon the OpenLST radio design released open-source
by Planet Labs from their Dove satellite constellation program.

The Dove Low-Speed Transceiver (LST) accumulated over 200 cumulative years of on-orbit data
across more than 150 satellites. SpaceCommsKit carries the open-source spirit of OpenLST
forward in firmware and ground station software, while building on the foundation with a
modern RF front end designed for today's supply chain and frequency landscape.

This isn't just another RF development kit. The SCK-915 is the evolution of a radio design
that flew in space — made accessible, kept open-source where it matters, and modernized
for today's hardware landscape.

Boards are hand-assembled in Tennessee, USA using components sourced exclusively
from authorized distributors (Digi-Key, Mouser, or manufacturer direct).
Ships to United States only. Boards are not conformal coated.

## Products — SpaceCommsKit Explorer

- Single Board: $500 USD — one SCK-915 radio board with antenna, bootloader flashed
- Ground Station: $750 USD — board + custom 3D printed stand + power switch + 5V 3A adapter
- Explorer Dev Kit: $1,500 USD — two boards (HWID 0001 + 0004) + full accessories

## SCK-915 Key Technical Specifications

- RF Frequency: 915 MHz (US ISM band — FCC Part 15.247, no license required)
- Modulation: 2-FSK with Forward Error Correction (FEC)
- Data Rate: 7.4–250 kbaud
- TX Power: +27 dBm
- Receiver Sensitivity: -118 dBm @ 1.2 kbaud
- Microcontroller: Texas Instruments CC1110
- RF Front End: Texas Instruments CC1190 (850-950MHz, active production)
- Supply Voltage: 5V DC maximum
- Boards are not conformal coated

## Ground Station Software — v1.2.0

The OpenLST Ground Station is a free Windows application (C# .NET 8, open source GPLv3)
included with every kit and available for free download without purchasing hardware.

Key features:
- Live GPS + barometric telemetry on Google Maps (fused 8-field beacon every 10 seconds)
- Flight recorder — saves .sckflight and .kml on every session
- Flight replay — animated map, altitude profile chart, event timeline, variable speed 1x-60x
- Remote SD card file management over RF
- OTA firmware flash over RF (AES-signed, page-level verification)
- Custom command framework for CC1110 and Pico
- 8 application tabs: Home, Commands, Custom Commands, GPS/Map, Files, Firmware, Terminal, Provision

Mission V1 real flight data (.sckflight file) is available for free download at
spacecommskit.com/mission-v1 — users can load a real stratospheric balloon flight
into the ground station replay form before purchasing any hardware.

## SCK-PBL-1 Payload Board

The SCK-PBL-1 is a Raspberry Pi Pico W carrier board for the complete HAB payload stack:
- OV2640 camera (SPI0)
- MicroSD slot (SPI0 shared)
- GPS6MV2 (NEO-6M) header — UART1, airborne mode configured at boot (rated to 50km)
- MS5611 barometric altimeter — I2C1, aviation grade, 10-1200 hPa (rated above 45km)
- SCK-915 interface — UART0
- 5 status LEDs (camera, SD, GPS, altimeter, fault) + 2 power indicators
- Power input: 9-12V DC via barrel jack, screw terminals, or JST-PH LiPo connector
- Full bridge rectifier — any polarity protection
- L7805 5V regulation + TC1262 3.3V LDO
- Onboard power switch

## HAB Mission Capability

The SCK-915 + SCK-PBL-1 stack is a complete high altitude balloon (HAB) mission platform.
No radio license required — 915MHz ISM band.

- Target altitude: ~30km (stratosphere)
- GPS airborne mode: NEO-6M rated to 50km with UBX CFG-NAV5 airborne mode
- Altimeter: MS5611 aviation grade, rated above 45km
- Recovery: SPOT Trace satellite tracker + AirTag last-mile precision
- Complete mission cost: ~$400 including recovery hardware
- FAA: 14 CFR Part 101 Subpart B exemption (<6 lbs payload, no license needed)
- Complete step-by-step mission guide at spacecommskit.com/hab-mission.html

## Mission V1

Mission V1 is SpaceCommsKit's first stratospheric balloon flight using the SCK-915 +
SCK-PBL-1 stack. Real flight data including .sckflight, .kml, and photos from altitude
are available at spacecommskit.com/mission-v1.html for free download.

## Open Source Status

- Firmware (CC1110): Open source — GPLv3 — github.com/eor123/spacecommskit
- Ground Station (C# .NET 8): Open source — GPLv3 — github.com/eor123/spacecommskit
- Pico Firmware (MicroPython): Open source — GPLv3 — github.com/eor123/spacecommskit
- SCK-915 Hardware Design: Proprietary SpaceCommsKit IP — not published
- SCK-PBL-1 Hardware Design: Proprietary SpaceCommsKit IP — not published

## OpenLST Heritage

Based on the open-source OpenLST design by Planet Labs (GPLv3).
Original project: github.com/OpenLST/openlst
SpaceCommsKit is not affiliated with Planet Labs.

- Mission V1 Data: https://www.spacecommskit.com/mission-v1.html
